CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE

Off Calendar Jan-10-2013 Vacated

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-20-2012 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Dec-09-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jan-15-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Kevin F. Woodall, Esq., 415.439.4803, for plaintiff Alfredo Plascencia.
Mark C. Thomas, Esq,. 415.986.1338 X140, for defendant All Seas Wholesale Inc.

RULING: Matter argued. At conclusion of argument, The Court adopted its tentative ruling as to all causes of action except #6 as follows: The Court denies summary judgment. The Court grants and denies summary adjudication in part. The Court denies summary adjudication as to the 1st, 2nd, 3rd, and 5th causes of action. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether Defendant misclassified Plaintiff as an exempt employee when plaintiff worked as the quality control person. Plaintiff's deposition states that: (1) Plaintiff stopped supervising people (79:12-14); (2) Plaintiff no longer had the authority to order people around (79:25-80:5); (3) the quality control job duties included driving a truck and picking up fish (85:21-86:11), unloading the fish (88:24-89:10), processing seafood orders and doing maintenance (89:24-90:15), and Plaintiff did not supervise any employees (90:16-18) or have input on employee schedules (131:21-23). Defendant's President also admitted that Plaintiff's duties changed to picking up product, loading trucks, and taking care of the shellfish room when he became the quality control person. (Ex. 19 attached to Howse Depo.) The Court denies summary adjudication on the 4th cause of action because Plaintiff demonstrates injury, see Jaimez v. DAIOHS, USA (2010) 181 Cal.App.4th 1286, 1306, and Defendant's constitutional/punitive damages cases are inapposite. The Court denies summary adjudication on the 7th cause of action because a UCL claim is a recognized means to recover unpaid wages. See Cortez v. Puralator Air Filtration Products Co. (2000) 23 Cal. 4th 163, 179; Janik v. Rudy, Exelrod & Zieff (2004) 119 Cal.App.4th 930, 936. The Court denies summary adjudication on the 8th cause of action. Plaintiff has standing to maintain the claim as an aggrieved employee. Defendant fails to shift the burden of demonstrating that other employees were not harmed by Defendant's conduct.

The Court takes the matter UNDER SUBMISSION AS TO THE 6TH CAUSE OF ACTION ONLY.

Counsel for defendant is ordered to prepare a proposed order reflecting the Court's decision.
Judge: Marla Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: LaVena Ward #7077.
